title: podman takes a lot of time to pull the images
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
I am trying to pull some image from quay.io or docker.io registries but it is slow and ends up with ```unexpected EOF``` error

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. install podman version 3.1.0-dev

2. run podman pull command 
```bash
$ podman pull docker.io/rust:latest
```
3. wait and see it will fail 

**Describe the results you received:**

![Screenshot from 2021-05-10 10-29-32](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8758274/117615390-ba9e6000-b17a-11eb-9060-4ef96a67fc1b.png)



**Describe the results you expected:**
it should have the image saved in the local images of podman

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I have installed podman via installation script of undercloud openstack
**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.1.0-dev
API Version:  3.1.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.16.1
Built:        Fri Mar 26 18:32:03 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  cpus: 4

      size: 65536
  kernel: 4.18.0-301.1.el8.x86_64

  ociRuntime:

    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.2-dev'
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /tmp/podman-run-1001/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_NET_RAW,CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true
    selinuxEnabled: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**
Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
![Screenshot from 2021-05-10 10-31-54](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8758274/117615806-4fa15900-b17b-11eb-91ec-dfa2de05b84a.png)

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

It's curious that the script install `3.1.0-dev`.  Is your node bandwidth limited?  It looks like a network error.

> Thanks for reaching out.
> 
> It's curious that the script installs `3.1.0-dev`. Is your node bandwidth limited? It looks like a network error.

Yes, this is running on a virtual machine having 2 Network bridges, Could that be a problem?
Network Bridges List is given below.
![Screenshot from 2021-05-10 12-57-08](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8758274/117633704-5afe7f80-b18f-11eb-97d8-5186bf217040.png)
Here is the specification of the bridge

![bridge-specs](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8758274/117635654-1247c600-b191-11eb-9f13-68ef81a50d8a.png)

I think 3.1.0-dev might still be the latest in the stream repos - we need
to check that and do something about it if so.

On Mon, May 10, 2021 at 04:56 Zeeshan Haider ***@***.***>
wrote:

> that be a problem?

Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub <[#10285 (comment)](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10285#issuecomment-836416171)>, or unsubscribe <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCB2A537ULWUIYBGITTTM6NR7ANCNFSM44QMHHVA> .

Can't upgrade or downgrade with ```yum``` or ```dnf``` don't have a clue how to proceed, but one thing is sure 3.1.0-dev is problematic
Podman v3.2.0 will be released this week and will hit CentOS Stream soon (Cc: @jnovy).  I am going to close the issue.  Please reopen if the issue persists after the update.
